description: Organ transplantation must stay scoped to successful clinical transplantation anchored by the 1954 Boston kidney transplant, not cold-chain preservation logistics or broad physiology lineage.

checks.reason: The node is anchored to the first successful human organ transplant, the 1954 Boston kidney transplant between identical twins.
Safe major surgery enabled organ replacement procedures without being unique to transplantation.
Antibiotics are retained as perioperative infection-control support for modern transplant surgery.
Immune rejection and immune suppression are central to transplant feasibility beyond identical-twin cases.
Mechanical refrigeration belongs on organ-preservation or deceased-donor scaling nodes, not the 1954 living-donor clinical transplant achievement.
Harvey's circulation discovery is broad physiology lineage rather than a direct clinical transplant prerequisite.
